message:
chore(beads): close bd-27du, open bd-1xbr for broad unbounded-collection audit

Close bd-27du and sub-beads bd-27du.5/10/11 tracking the initial
audit_log/events Vec capacity guard work. Create new bead bd-1xbr
to track the broad sweep capping remaining unbounded collections
across security, runtime, connector, vef, and tools subsystems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1xao","title":"[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 13

Task Objective:
Track and gate production-grade adoption of impossible-by-default capabilities.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 'Impossible-by-default' capabilities are defined: operations that are blocked unless explicitly enabled with cryptographic authorization.
2. At minimum, these capabilities are impossible-by-default: (a) arbitrary file system access outside project root, (b) outbound network to non-allowlisted hosts, (c) spawning child processes without sandbox, (d) loading unsigned extensions, (e) disabling hardening profiles.
3. Each impossible-by-default capability requires explicit opt-in via signed capability token with expiry.
4. Attempting a blocked operation produces a clear, actionable error message (not a generic permission denied).
5. Adoption metric: >= 90% of production deployments run with all impossible-by-default capabilities enforced (measured via telemetry).
6. No impossible-by-default capability can be silently disabled; any disabling is logged and alerted.
7. Evidence: capability_enforcement_report.json listing each capability, enforcement status, and opt-in rate.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:34.668279352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590071518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590043385Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xao","depends_on_id":"bd-pga7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:25.443838167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xbc","title":"[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Why This Exists:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Task Objective:
Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/time_travel_runtime.md`, `src/replay/time_travel_engine.rs`, `tests/lab/time_travel_replay_equivalence.rs`, `artifacts/10.17/time_travel_replay_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:03.099102362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:57.447111352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:57.447089121Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-17","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xbc","depends_on_id":"bd-274s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:18.349690460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1xpi","title":"Fix 2 fail-open boundaries + 6 counter overflow gaps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-26T02:18:42.723723566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T02:30:04.094967864Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T02:30:04.094943910Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1xtf","title":"[10.16] Migrate existing or planned relevant TUI workflows to `frankentui` primitives.","description":"## Why This Exists

Once the frankentui integration contract (bd-34ll) is defined, existing or planned console/TUI workflows in franken_node must be migrated to use frankentui primitives. This eliminates duplicate homegrown TUI rendering stacks and ensures all operator-visible surfaces go through a single, testable, styled presentation layer. Any remaining homegrown terminal rendering after this bead is a policy violation.

In the three-kernel architecture, franken_node's operator experience is a first-class concern. Migrating to frankentui ensures consistent rendering semantics, accessibility compliance, and deterministic snapshot testing across all TUI surfaces.

## What This Must Do

1. Inventory all current and planned TUI/console output points in franken_node:
   - `src/cli.rs` — CLI command output (status displays, table formatting, progress indicators).
   - Any `println!`/`eprintln!`/`write!` to stdout/stderr in `src/connector/`, `src/control_plane/`, `src/runtime/`, `src/security/`.
   - Error display surfaces fed by `src/connector/error_code_registry.rs`.
   - Planned interactive surfaces (dashboards, live status views).

2. Migrate each identified surface to frankentui abstraction points:
   - Replace raw `println!`/formatting with frankentui component calls.
   - Wire styling through frankentui token system (no raw ANSI codes).
   - Integrate event-loop as specified in the bd-34ll contract.

3. Create `tests/integration/frankentui_surface_migration.rs`:
   - Integration tests that verify each migrated surface renders through frankentui.
   - Tests that detect any remaining raw terminal output bypassing frankentui.
   - Snapshot assertions for key surfaces.

4. Generate `artifacts/10.16/frankentui_surface_inventory.csv` with columns:
   - `module_path, surface_name, migration_status, frankentui_component, notes`.
   - All rows must show `migration_status = "complete"` at gate time.

5. Create verification script `scripts/check_frankentui_migration.py` with `--json` flag and `self_test()`:
   - Scans source for raw terminal output patterns (e.g., `println!`, raw ANSI escapes) in modules classified as frankentui-mandatory.
   - Validates inventory CSV completeness.

6. Create `tests/test_check_frankentui_migration.py` with unit tests.

7. Produce evidence artifacts:
   - `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-1xtf/verification_evidence.json`
   - `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-1xtf/verification_summary.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Relevant workflows use `frankentui` abstraction points; no duplicate homegrown TUI stack remains in migrated surfaces.
- The surface inventory CSV has zero rows with `migration_status != "complete"`.
- Source scanning finds zero raw ANSI escape sequences in frankentui-mandatory modules.
- Integration tests pass for all migrated surfaces.
- No `println!` or `eprintln!` calls remain in modules classified as mandatory-frankentui in the dependency map (bd-28ld).

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- **Unit tests**: Validate inventory CSV schema, migration status completeness, raw-output scanning logic.
- **Integration tests**: Each migrated surface renders correctly through frankentui; snapshot comparison with approved baselines.
- **Event codes**: `FRANKENTUI_SURFACE_MIGRATED` (info), `FRANKENTUI_RAW_OUTPUT_DETECTED` (error), `FRANKENTUI_MIGRATION_INCOMPLETE` (warning).
- **Trace correlation**: Surface name included in all migration-related log events.
- **Deterministic replay**: Snapshot tests must produce identical output given identical terminal dimensions and input.

## Expected Artifacts

- `tests/integration/frankentui_surface_migration.rs`
- `artifacts/10.16/frankentui_surface_inventory.csv`
- `scripts/check_frankentui_migration.py`
- `tests/test_check_frankentui_migration.py`
- `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-1xtf/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-1xtf/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies

- **bd-34ll** (blocks): The frankentui integration contract must be defined before migration can begin.

## Dependents

- **bd-1719**: Snapshot/interaction tests depend on migrated surfaces existing.
- **bd-10g0**: Section gate depends on this bead.","acceptance_criteria":"- Relevant workflows use `frankentui` abstraction points; no duplicate homegrown TUI stack remains in migrated surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:01.770888002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T21:08:28.459813721Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T21:08:28.459773015Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-16","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xtf","depends_on_id":"bd-34ll","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:05:11.175448186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-273","title":"[10.4] Implement extension certification levels tied to policy controls.","description":"## Why This Exists

Extension certification levels create a tiered trust hierarchy that maps directly to policy controls. Not all extensions need the same level of scrutiny: a formatting utility with no network access has different risk characteristics than a deployment orchestrator with full filesystem and network capabilities. Certification levels formalize this distinction into enforceable policy tiers.

Certification ties together provenance (bd-1ah), reputation (bd-ml1), and manifest capabilities (bd-1gx) into a single policy-actionable classification. Each certification level enables or restricts specific capabilities, deployment contexts, and operational permissions. This is the policy bridge between the trust data (what we know about an extension) and the enforcement layer (what an extension is allowed to do).

## What This Must Do

1. Define certification levels (e.g., Uncertified, Basic, Standard, Verified, Audited) with clear criteria for each level including: minimum provenance level, minimum publisher reputation tier, required capability declarations, required test coverage evidence, and optional third-party audit attestation.
2. Implement the certification evaluation engine: given an extension's manifest, provenance, and publisher reputation, compute the certification level it qualifies for.
3. Map certification levels to policy controls: which capabilities are allowed at each level, which deployment contexts (dev/staging/production) permit which levels, and what runtime restrictions apply per level.
4. Implement certification promotion workflow: extensions can be promoted to higher certification levels by satisfying additional criteria (e.g., providing reproducible build evidence for Standard -> Verified, third-party audit attestation for Verified -> Audited).
5. Implement certification demotion: certification level is automatically reduced when underlying trust data degrades (e.g., publisher reputation drops below threshold, provenance attestation expires).
6. Define the certification registry: a queryable store of extension certification levels with change history.
7. Integrate certification levels into trust cards (bd-2yh) as a primary trust signal.
8. Implement certification-level-based policy gates in the extension admission pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

- At least 4 certification levels are defined with non-overlapping criteria.
- Certification evaluation is deterministic: same inputs produce identical level assignments.
- Policy control mapping is complete: every certification level has explicit capability allow/deny lists.
- Certification promotion requires explicit evidence submission (not automatic).
- Certification demotion triggers automatically on trust data degradation with notification to publisher.
- Certification registry maintains complete change history with signed entries.
- Certification levels are displayed in trust cards and queryable via API/CLI.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: certification evaluation with edge cases at tier boundaries, promotion/demotion logic, policy mapping completeness.
- Integration tests: full evaluation pipeline (manifest + provenance + reputation -> certification level -> policy gate enforcement).
- E2E tests: extension submission with various trust profiles -> certification assignment -> capability enforcement.
- Adversarial tests: attempts to maintain certification after trust degradation, gaming promotion criteria.
- Structured logs: CERTIFICATION_EVALUATED, CERTIFICATION_ASSIGNED, CERTIFICATION_PROMOTED (with evidence reference), CERTIFICATION_DEMOTED (with reason), CERTIFICATION_POLICY_ENFORCED, CERTIFICATION_GATE_PASS, CERTIFICATION_GATE_REJECT. All with trace IDs and extension identity.

## Expected Artifacts

- `docs/specs/section_10_4/bd-273_contract.md` — certification levels spec
- `src/supply_chain/certification.rs` — Rust types for certification model
- `scripts/check_certification_levels.py` — verification script with `--json` and `self_test()`
- `tests/test_check_certification_levels.py` — unit tests for verification script
- `artifacts/section_10_4/bd-273/verification_evidence.json` — CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_4/bd-273/verification_summary.md` — outcome summary

## Dependencies

- **bd-ml1** (blocks this) — publisher reputation is an input to certification evaluation
- **bd-1ah** (blocks this) — provenance level is an input to certification evaluation
- **bd-1gx** (blocks this) — manifest capabilities define what needs to be certified
- Blocks: bd-261k (section gate), bd-1xg (plan tracker)","acceptance_criteria":"1. Certification levels: Uncertified, Self-Certified, Community-Reviewed, Audited, Formally-Verified. Each level has explicit requirements and capability gates.
2. Policy controls per level: Uncertified extensions cannot access NetworkEgress or ProcessSpawn capabilities. Self-Certified can access sandboxed filesystem only. Audited and above can access full capability set per manifest declaration.
3. Certification level is determined by: attestation chain completeness (bd-1ah), publisher reputation tier (bd-ml1), automated audit pass/fail, and optional human review attestation.
4. Level transitions require: (a) meeting all entry criteria for the target level, (b) passing automated verification checks, (c) for Audited+, a signed attestation from an authorized reviewer.
5. Policy enforcement is fail-closed: an extension whose certification level does not meet the policy requirement for a requested capability is denied with a structured error explaining the gap and the path to certification.
6. Certification status is displayed on the trust card (bd-2yh) and consumed by the admission pipeline (10.13 FCP) for install-time gating.
7. CLI surface: `franken-node certify <ext-id> --level <level>` (with appropriate authorization), `franken-node certification status <ext-id>` (query current level and requirements for next level).
8. All certification operations emit structured log events: CERTIFICATION_GRANTED, CERTIFICATION_DENIED, CERTIFICATION_REVOKED, POLICY_GATE_PASSED, POLICY_GATE_DENIED with extension ID, level, and trace IDs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:45.904492560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:10:37.080208195Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T20:10:37.080172779Z","close_reason":"Completed: Extension certification levels with 5 levels (uncertified/basic/standard/verified/audited), capability policy matrix, deployment context gates, promotion/demotion workflows, hash-chained audit trail. 19 Rust inline tests, 25 Python verification tests, 12/12 verification checks all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-4","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-273","depends_on_id":"bd-1ah","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:16:37.747417416Z","created_by":"CrimsonCrane","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-273","depends_on_id":"bd-1gx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:13:38.127639493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-273","depends_on_id":"bd-ml1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:16:32.632929210Z","created_by":"CrimsonCrane","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-274s","title":"[10.17] Implement Bayesian adversary graph and automated quarantine controller.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Why This Exists:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Task Objective:
Implement Bayesian adversary graph and automated quarantine controller.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Risk posterior updates are deterministic from identical evidence; policy thresholds trigger reproducible control actions (throttle/isolate/revoke/quarantine) with signed evidence entries.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/security/adversary_graph.rs`, `src/security/quarantine_controller.rs`, `tests/integration/bayesian_risk_quarantine.rs`, `artifacts/10.17/adversary_graph_state.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-274s/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-274s/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.17] Implement Bayesian adversary graph and automated quarantine controller.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.17] Implement Bayesian adversary graph and automated quarantine controller." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.17] Implement Bayesian adversary graph and automated quarantine controller." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.17] Implement Bayesian adversary graph and automated quarantine controller." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.17] Implement Bayesian adversary graph and automated quarantine controller." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Risk posterior updates are deterministic from identical evidence; policy thresholds trigger reproducible control actions (throttle/isolate/revoke/quarantine) with signed evidence entries.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkLantern","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:03.008093682Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:37.005541031Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:37.005495897Z","close_reason":"Implemented adversary_graph + quarantine_controller, added integration fixture/artifacts, checker PASS 17/17, unit tests PASS","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-17","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-274s","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:18.307975166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-274s","author":"CrimsonLynx","text":"CrimsonLynx support slice (non-overlapping): added deterministic integration fixture + evidence artifacts at tests/integration/bayesian_risk_quarantine.rs, crates/franken-node/tests/bayesian_risk_quarantine.rs, artifacts/10.17/adversary_graph_state.json, artifacts/section_10_17/bd-274s/verification_evidence.json, artifacts/section_10_17/bd-274s/verification_summary.md.
